name: South Carolina Dental Assistant Registration
description: Complete guide to dental assistant requirements in South Carolina — fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.

provider.name: South Carolina State Board of Dentistry

address.description: SC Board of Dentistry, Post Office Box 11329, Columbia, SC 29211-1329

requiredDocuments.name: Proof of Training
requiredDocuments.description: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.

requiredDocuments.name: DANB Certification
requiredDocuments.description: Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.

requiredDocuments.name: Radiography Certification
requiredDocuments.description: Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

mainEntity.name: Does South Carolina require a dental assistant license?

acceptedAnswer.text: No, South Carolina does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. However, expanded functions and radiography may require permits or certifications.

mainEntity.name: Is DANB certification recognized in South Carolina?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in South Carolina.

mainEntity.name: Can I take dental x-rays in South Carolina?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

mainEntity.name: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in South Carolina?

acceptedAnswer.text: There is no state application fee for general dental assistants in South Carolina. However, DANB CDA exam costs $450, and radiography certifications may have additional fees.
